div.span: ACL-Based Persistence
Backdoor Persistence
Certificate Persistence
Ticket-Based Persistence
Credential Persistence
AdminSDHolder Abuse
ACL-Based

Requirements:
- Domain Admin access

Method:
Add ACE to AdminSDHolder
Grant controlled account
GenericAll or WriteDACL

Result:
Permissions propagate to
protected groups every 60 min

Persistence:
+ Invisible to group membership
+ Auto-propagates to admins
+ Survives in 60-min window
- SDProp removes after detection
- Requires AdminCount manipulation
DCSync Rights

Requirements:
- WriteDACL on domain object

Method:
Add-DomainObjectAcl
Grant DS-Replication rights
to controlled user

Result:
User can perform DCSync
Extract all domain credentials

Persistence:
+ Standard user appearance
+ No group membership needed
+ Difficult to detect
- ACL auditing may catch
- Requires ACL modification rights
GPO Delegation

Requirements:
- Permissions to create/modify GPO

Method:
Create GPO with backdoor
Link to high-value OUs
Deploy tasks/scripts

Result:
Code execution on all
machines in OU scope

Persistence:
+ Legitimate AD mechanism
+ Wide deployment scope
+ Executes on schedule
- GPO changes logged
- Admins may review GPOs
Skeleton Key
Backdoor-Based

Requirements:
- Domain Controller access
- Mimikatz on DC

Method:
mimikatz misc::skeleton
Patches LSASS on DC

Result:
Master password works for all users
Original passwords still valid

Persistence:
+ Universal access
+ No account modifications
- Requires DC reboot to persist
- Detected by DC reboot
- Very risky on production DC
Directory Service Restore Mode DSRM

Requirements:
- Domain Controller access
- Local admin on DC

Method:
Change DSRM password
Enable network logon
reg add HKLM\\System\\...\\Lsa
/v DsrmAdminLogonBehavior /t REG_DWORD /d 2

Result:
Local admin account on DC
Works for network authentication

Persistence:
+ Independent of domain
+ Survives domain password changes
+ Rarely monitored
- Single DC scope
- Registry change detectable
Malicious SPN

Requirements:
- Permissions to modify
user attributes

Method:
Add SPN to user account
Kerberoast later for access

Result:
Hidden service account
Can extract via Kerberoasting

Persistence:
+ Very stealthy
+ Blends with legitimate SPNs
- Requires SPN enumeration
- Password rotation breaks it
Forged Certificates
Certificate-Based

Requirements:
- Vulnerable cert template OR
- CA private key

Method:
Certify.exe or Certipy
Request cert with admin SAN
OR steal CA cert + key

Result:
Certificate valid for authentication
Can request TGTs indefinitely

Persistence:
+ Credential-agnostic longest
+ Survives password changes
+ Valid until cert expiration
+ Hardest to remediate
- Requires CA compromise or vuln
- Certificate revocation removes access
Golden Ticket
Ticket-Based

Requirements:
- krbtgt account hash
- Domain SID

Method:
mimikatz kerberos::golden
/user:fakeadmin /domain:X
/sid:S-1-5-... /krbtgt:hash

Result:
Forged TGT valid for 10 years
Impersonate any user/group

Persistence:
+ Long lifetime default 10 years
+ Works even if account deleted
+ Bypasses account policies
- Detected by krbtgt rotation
- Requires re-creation after rotation
Silver Ticket

Requirements:
- Service account hash
- Service SPN
- Domain SID

Method:
mimikatz kerberos::golden
/service:cifs /target:server
/rc4:hash /sid:S-1-5-...

Result:
Forged TGS for specific service
30-day default lifetime

Persistence:
+ Stealthier than Golden Ticket
+ No KDC contact needed
+ Service-specific access
- Limited to one service
- Shorter lifetime than Golden
Diamond Ticket

Requirements:
- Valid user credentials
- Access to DC

Method:
Request real TGT
Decrypt with krbtgt hash
Modify PAC
Re-encrypt with krbtgt

Result:
Modified legitimate ticket
Appears genuine to detection

Persistence:
+ Evades Golden Ticket detection
+ Uses real ticket structure
+ Harder to distinguish
- More complex to create
- Still requires krbtgt hash
DCSync Attack
Credential-Based

Requirements:
- Replication rights OR
- Domain Admin access

Method:
mimikatz lsadump::dcsync
/domain:X /user:all

Result:
All domain password hashes
NTLM & Kerberos keys

Persistence:
+ Multiple admin credentials
+ Service account hashes
- Detected if passwords change
- Expires with rotation
Local Admin Access

Maintain admin on:
- Multiple workstations
- Servers with services
- Jump boxes

Result:
Lateral movement capability
Credential harvesting

Persistence:
+ Access across infrastructure
+ Multiple entry points
- Account-dependent
- Limited to machine scope
Domain Compromise Achieved
Choose Persistence Strategy
Comparison
Best Choice?

Stealth: Certificate-based
Reason: Credential-agnostic,
long lifetime, hard to detect

Reliability: Golden Ticket
Reason: Works indefinitely,
bypasses all controls

Low-Privilege: ACL manipulation
Reason: Doesn't require
high privileges to maintain

Recommended: Layered approach
Use multiple methods
for redundancy

DETECTION & REMEDIATION:
Golden Ticket: Rotate krbtgt twice - Monitor Event 4769
Certificates: Revoke certs - Rotate CA private key
ACL: Regular ACL audits - Monitor Event 5136 object mods
Backdoors: Regular DC integrity checks - Monitor registry changes
